x-jql.doc: one grain query: source + filter + time + select; nests as a cross-grain filter
every filter matches (AND)
spans below a matching span in the trace tree
spans above a matching span in the trace tree
at least one filter matches (OR)
at least one child span matches
every child span matches
no child span matches
at least one trace in the session matches
every trace in the session matches
no trace in the session matches
at least k related rows match — between any_ (k=1) and every_
structured attributes
spans cited as evidence by a judge
span cost — span grain
duration in ms
compare any valid grain field with a literal
compare any valid grain field with a literal
compare any valid grain field with a literal
compare any valid grain field with a literal
compare any valid grain field with a literal
compare any valid grain field with a literal
a column reference operand
substring search
raw fact-store judge scores
config-store judge lookup
LLM model name — span grain
span name — span grain
the filter does not match (NOT)
an aggregate over a row's child spans meets a threshold
an aggregate over a session's traces meets a threshold
an aggregate over a row's judge scores meets a threshold (no where)

OverAgg.description:
The aggregate an over_* predicate compares — deliberately narrower than AggExpr
(no per/quantile/nested where; see compile._agg_over).
OverAgg.title: OverAgg

x-jql.doc: regex search
trace/span status
fast whole-word content search
add computed columns to every row (rows unchanged)
x-jql.example: .pipe().derive({ share: arith('div', col('cost'), aggExpr({ func: 'sum', field: 'cost', per: 'trace' })) })

x-jql.doc: an aggregate expression; `per` makes it a per-group window, `where` a conditional aggregate
arithmetic over expressions
x-jql.example: arith('div', col('cost'), aggExpr({ func: 'sum', field: 'cost', per: 'trace' }))

x-jql.doc: keep the first/last n rows per group (adds rank column `_rn`)
order the rows
collapse to one row per group with computed aggregates (grain change)
time-bucket key for summarize (outputs column `bucket`)
keep the first n rows (after sort), optionally skipping offset rows
keep rows passing a comparison (after summarize: HAVING)
one scalar `value`; `{ func, field, q? }`, where `q` is required for `quantile`
a total, or per-group counts: `.count('status')`
matching IDs — cap with the request-level `limit` option
positional rows globally or within a field; `{ within?, by?, pick? }`
the n most recent rows
rows: `.rows({ fields: ['trace_id', 'status'], limit: 100 })`
the n largest by a numeric field: `.top(20, 'duration')`
time buckets; `{ metric: 'count' | 'rate', bucket: '1d' }`
catalog lookup: judge names, span names, models, ...
variants: standard fields usable for filters, groups, and aggregates
spans cited by a judge decision

x-jql.doc: chart-ready rows with explicit axes and a bounded chart type
x-jql.example: .chart({ chartType: 'bar', title: 'Cost by model', xAxis: { key: 'model' }, yAxis: { key: 'cost' } })

x-jql.doc: table-ready rows projected to an explicit ordered column list
